sections.value: (2024) PICS-2024 - 5th Ph.D. school in the series of Foundations of Programming and Software Systems. 💡 PhD school that crosses many disciplines with speakers from diverse research areas to deliver their stories on use of probability in computer science. ⏱ 14.-18. October, 2024 📝 English 📍 IT University of Copenhagen (Denmark) 💡 Apparently, the budget could not cover printing event posters or signs- but credit where credit is due, once they started serving pastries during the breaks, everyone's spirit rose to the skies ;) 💡 The Spandauer (sweet pastry) alone are worth the travel to Denmark! :D 💬 I am glad that Ānrán invited me to stray a bit from my usual NLP-related activities and it was fun to meet and talk with so many people that actually do know 'how to math' :D

(2024) EXPO-2024 - Student's projects 💡 A yearly event for students to display and discuss the outcomes of their projects and thesises. ⏱ 27. March, 2024 📝 German & English 📍 BASE.CAMP creative lab at Informatikum, Hamburg, Germany ⭐ (Poster Presentation) CRAMT: Cross-Lingual Resource Aggregation of Low-Resource Machine Translation and Metadata Here we presented the current state of our tool which grew out of the MTACR project that we (Tania, Myy, Me, Ānrán, Raman) are working on together. ⭐ (Second Place) CRAMT at EXPO 2024 We were awarded the second place of all presented projects and even received a price of 200€ :D

(2024) Spring Hackathon with CHECK24 💡 A reoccurring Hackathon in which you are meant to 'Unleash your creativity. Work in a team. Learn from industry experts.' ⏱ 26. March, 2024 📝 German 📍 Hamburg University of Applied Sciences (HAW Hamburg), Hamburg, Germany ⭐ (Third Place) 'Crazy Teaching' at Spring Hackathon with CHECK24 We were awarded the third place for our spontaneous creation and received some T-Shirts to serve as free advertisement ;) 💬 Raman invited us (Tania, Ānrán, and me) to this event and it was a lot of fun to cluelessly jump into a short project, trying to merge different ideas and ways of working in a (partially) new and unknown group!

(2024) ESSV-2024 - 35th. Conference on Electronic Speech Signal Processing 💡 Conference spanning a wide range of fields that concern the creation, analysis, and (digital) processing of speech signals, with a special focus on applied research, i.e. practical applications and technologies. ⏱ 06.-08. March, 2024 📝 English & German 📍 Eastern Bavarian Technical University of Applied Sciences Regensburg, Regensburg, Germany ⭐ (Paper Presentation) Can We See Your Response Before You Speak? Exploring Linguistic Information Found In Inter-Utterance Pauses. Here I presented the paper about processing pause contents that we (Christian Schuler, Shravan Nayak, Debjoy Saha, Timo Baumann) had submitted.

(2023) Digital Total - Computing & Data Science an der Universität Hamburg und in der Wissenschaftsmetropole Hamburg 💡 Conference aimed at publicly highlight and consolidate the important data science/AI/computation themes in Hamburg's academic scene. ⏱ 09.-10. October, 2023 📝 English & German 📍 House of Computing & Data Science, Hamburg, Germany 💬 Yet another fun event on which Domi and I attended many interesting talks inbetween our cake-breaks! :D

(2023) EASIER Autumn School - Sign language data meets data science - data science meets sign linguistics 💡 Workshop aimed at bridging the gap between data scientists and sign language experts, advancing collaborative endevors. ⏱ 25.-26. September, 2023 📝 English & International Sign Language 📍 Institute of German Sign Language and Communication of the Deaf, Hamburg, Germany 💬 I got to meet many amazingly kind people, learned a lot from the given talks by experts and saw very inspiring project-posters that left me with way too many ideas to follow up on :)

(2023) TURN Prototyp Zukunft - Politik und Praxis für innovative Lehre 💡 Conference aimed at sharing and collectively developing innovative teaching concepts for the education of the future. ⏱ 13.-15. September, 2023 📝 German 📍 TH Köln - University of Applied Sciences, Cologne, Germany 💬 This was the first time for Dominik and me visiting Cologne and attending a conference together and just the cake alone would have been worth the travel! :D ⭐ (Pitch Session) Data-driven Digital Innovation Lab Here I presented inside a 'Pitch Session' our research project Data-driven Digital Innovation Lab (Christian Schuler, Dominik Hauser, Stephan Leible, Maren Gierlich-Joas)

(2023) 6th International Conference on Kurdish Linguistics 💡 Conference aimed at the low-resource language Kurdish and any neighboring or contacting languages with a linguistic focus. ⏱ 04.-05. September, 2023 📝 English 📍 Goethe Universität, Frankfurt am Main, Germany 💬 Here, Raman and I presented our projects as part of the poster session. We had an amazing time and got to meet so many awesome people that we can't wait to see again in two years at the 7th ICKL! :D ⭐ (Poster Session) Towards a Complete Mapping of Kurdish Dialectology (Christian Schuler, Raman Ahmad) → https://github.com/Low-ResourceDialectology/DialectMapping ⭐ (Poster Session) Analysis of Phonology and Morphology in the Kobani Dialect (Raman Ahmad, Christian Schuler) → https://github.com/Low-ResourceDialectology/KobaniAnalysis
